title: Water Sources and Delivery Systems Schema
description: Schema for structured data on water sources and water delivery systems across multiple locations.

locations.description: Array of location records, each representing a city, metro area, or county.

citation.description: Source citation for a data item.

page_number.description: Page number or range within the source document (optional).

volume_unit.description: Unit of measurement for volume or flow rate.

water_source.description: A single water source contributing to the system's supply.

source_name.description: Name of the water source (e.g., 'Colorado River', 'Edwards Aquifer', 'Salt River Project Reservoir System').

contributing_source_water.description: For engineered sources (reservoirs, canals), the originating natural source water (e.g., 'Salt River' for a reservoir fed by the Salt River). Null if not applicable or if the source is itself a primary natural source.

percentage_of_supply.description: Percentage of total current water demand fulfilled by this source. Null if unknown.

measured_volume.description: A volume or flow-rate value with its unit and source citation.

value.description: Numeric value. Null if data is unavailable.

location.description: Water system data for a single city, metro area, or county.

location_name.description: Name of the city, metro area, or county.

water_utility.description: The water utility or authority operating the delivery system.

utility_name.description: Official name of the water utility or water authority.

water_sources.description: All direct natural or engineered water sources supplying the system.

max_daily_treatment_capacity.description: Maximum total daily water treatment capacity across all water treatment plants in the system.

average_daily_demand.description: Average daily water demand served by the system.

peak_daily_demand.description: Peak daily water demand across all seasons of the year.

total_treated_storage_capacity.description: Total storage capacity for treated (finished) water in the system.
